```{r}
#| label: BTCM Nonsense
#| message: true
#| warning: true
#| include: false
#| paged-print: true


# Load packages

pacman::p_load("tidyverse", "janitor", "lubridate", "stringr", "forcats", "openxlsx", "tidyr", "fs", "readxl", "writexl")

# --- Helper formatters ---
# export: stable legacy format (dash-separated, four-digit year)
fmt_export <- function(x) {
  x_date <- as.Date(x)
  format(x_date, "%m-%d-%Y")
}

# display: requested format with zero-padded day (safe for parsing back to Date)
# results like "12/05/25"
fmt_display <- function(x, pad_day = TRUE) {
  x_date <- as.Date(x)
  mm <- format(x_date, "%m")
  dd <- if (pad_day) format(x_date, "%d") else sub("^0", "", format(x_date, "%d"))
  yy <- format(x_date, "%y")
  paste0(mm, "/", dd, "/", yy)
}

#find first date and last date of previous month
first_day_last_month <- floor_date(today(), unit="month") - months(3)
last_day_last_month <- floor_date(today(), unit="month")- months(2)-days(1) 


attendees_path <- "C:/Users/asokol/OneDrive - University of South Carolina/StudentLife/Data/Master Lists/SL Master Attendance Fall 25-Spring 26.xlsx"
events_path    <- "C:/Users/asokol/OneDrive - University of South Carolina/StudentLife/Data/BTCM Events Data/January 2026 BTCM Event Upload 20260415.xlsx"
sl_facstaff_path <- "C:/Users/asokol/OneDrive - University of South Carolina/StudentLife/Data/FacStaff Upload 20251203.xlsx"

# Output paths
out_dir <- "C:/Users/asokol/OneDrive - University of South Carolina/StudentLife/Data/BTCM Events Data/BTCM Event Data Project/AY2526 uploads/January 2026"
fs::dir_create(out_dir)

# --- Read in data ---
attendees_add <- read.xlsx(attendees_path, detectDates = TRUE, sep.names = " ") %>% 
  mutate(across(where(is.character), ~str_trim(.x, side = "both")))

Events_add <- read.xlsx(events_path, detectDates = TRUE, sep.names = " ")

SL_FacStaff <- read.xlsx(sl_facstaff_path, sep.names = " ") %>%
  mutate(Email = tolower(str_trim(Email)))

# --- Prep Events_add (parse dates, create export + display cols) ---
events_trunc <- Events_add %>%
  rename(`Event Code` = `Event ID`) %>%
  select(`Event Code`, `Start Date`, `End Date`, `Event Title`, Hosts, Categories) %>%
  mutate(
    # canonical date objects for filtering/logic
    EventDateStart = as.Date(`Start Date`),
    EventDateEnd   = as.Date(`End Date`),

    # stable export columns (kept for internal/reference)
    export_Start   = fmt_export(EventDateStart),
    export_End     = fmt_export(EventDateEnd),

    # presentation/display columns (no $ so they can be parsed back to Date)
    display_Start  = fmt_display(EventDateStart, pad_day = TRUE),
    display_End    = fmt_display(EventDateEnd, pad_day = TRUE),

    across(where(is.character), ~str_trim(.x, side = "both"))
  )

# combine data sets
EventsAttnd <- left_join(attendees_add, events_trunc, by = "Event Code") %>%
  rename(Event = `Event Title`)

# --- split Hosts/Categories into columns (as you had) ---
tmp <- EventsAttnd %>%
  separate_wider_delim(cols = Hosts, delim = "|", names_sep = "", too_few = "align_start", 
                       cols_remove = TRUE, names_repair = ~ sub("Hosts", "Host", .x)) %>%
  separate_wider_delim(cols = Categories, delim = "|", names_sep = "", 
                       too_few = "align_start", cols_remove = TRUE, names_repair = ~ sub("Categories", "Category", .x))

  host_cols <- grep("^Host", names(tmp), value = TRUE)
  cat_cols  <- grep("^Category", names(tmp), value = TRUE)


# --- clean and robust category mapping + other cleaning ---
Events_addCln <- tmp %>%
  # broadened date window to avoid accidentally dropping events; narrow as needed
  filter(`Start Date` >= first_day_last_month & `Start Date` <= last_day_last_month) %>%
  mutate(
    # normalized event title for consistent pattern checks
    Event_norm = str_squish(str_trim(Event)),
    Event_norm_lower = str_to_lower(Event_norm),

    # canonical cleaned event name (no punctuation) for filenames
    Event_RmPunct = str_remove_all(Event, "[^[:alnum:]\\s]"),

    # standardize characters
    across(where(is.character), str_trim),

    # Robust Category mapping (case-insensitive, host-aware, normalized outputs)
    Category = case_when(
      # Host-based matches (Dance Marathon, Homecoming)
      if_any(all_of(host_cols), ~ str_detect(str_trim(.x), regex("dance\\s*marathon", ignore_case = TRUE))) ~ "Dance_Marathon",
      if_any(all_of(host_cols), ~ str_detect(str_trim(.x), 
                                             regex("homecoming\\s*commission|homecoming", ignore_case = TRUE))) ~ "Homecoming",
      #event based matches
      str_detect(Event_norm, regex("^tiger\\s*burn", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Tiger_Burn",
      str_detect(Event_norm, regex(".*blood.*", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Carolina_Clemson_Blood_Drive",
      str_detect(Event_norm, regex("^service\\ssaturday", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Service_Saturday",
      str_detect(Event_norm, regex("^alternative\\s*break|^alternative.*Break.*", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Alternative_Break",
      str_detect(Event_norm, regex("^Live\\s*at|@\\s*the\\s*Underground", 
                                   ignore_case= TRUE)) ~ "Live_at_the_Underground",
      str_detect(Event_norm, regex("^MAPP.", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "MAPP",
      str_detect(Event_norm, regex("cfelp|close\\s*family\\s*emerging", 
                                   ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Close_Family_Emerging_Leaders_Program",
      str_detect(Event_norm, regex("^t.*\\s*a.*\\s*d.*|^tad", 
                                   ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Thursday_After_Dark",
      str_detect(Event_norm, regex("^TT.|^Tuesday\\s*Takeover.", 
                                   ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Tuesday_Takeover",
      str_detect(Event_norm, regex("empower", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "EmpowerMENt",
      # Production/Entertainment host matches
      if_any(all_of(host_cols), ~ str_detect(str_trim(.x), 
                                             regex("carolina\\s*productions", ignore_case = TRUE))) ~ "Carolina_Productions",
      if_any(all_of(host_cols), ~ str_detect(str_trim(.x), 
                                             regex("gamecock\\s*entertainment", ignore_case = TRUE))) ~ "Gamecock_Entertainment",

      # Fallback to Drop
      TRUE ~ "Drop"
    ),

    # clean affiliation variable
    Affiliation_Cln = case_when(
      str_starts(Affiliation, "F") ~ "Faculty/Staff",
      str_starts(Affiliation, "Sta") ~ "Faculty/Staff",
      TRUE ~ "Student"
    ),

    Campus_Email_cln = tolower(str_trim(`Campus Email`))
  ) %>%
  # left join to fac/staff list; keep lowercase email join
  left_join(SL_FacStaff, by = c("Campus_Email_cln" = "Email")) %>%
  # filter out Drops, optionally keep Faculty/Staff and non-USCID for debugging - adjust as needed
  filter(Category != "Drop") %>%
  # If you want to debug drops due to USCID or Faculty/Staff, comment the next two lines temporarily
  filter(Affiliation_Cln != "Faculty/Staff") %>%
  filter(!is.na(USCID)) %>%
  # select just the columns you need downstream
  select(Category, USCID, Event_RmPunct, export_Start, export_End, display_Start, display_End, EventDateStart, EventDateEnd) %>%
  rename(
    EVENT = Event_RmPunct,
    `START DATE` = export_Start,
    `END DATE` = export_End,
    DISPLAY_START = display_Start,
    DISPLAY_END = display_End
  )

# Note: we keep true EventDateStart/EventDateEnd (Date objects) in the dataset for Excel export
# If you prefer to drop the normalization columns, do so here.

# clean up intermediate objects
rm(attendees_add, Events_add, events_trunc, EventsAttnd, SL_FacStaff, tmp)

# --- Grouping / splitting into named lists and export ---
Dat_Group <- Events_addCln %>%
  group_by(Category, EVENT, `START DATE`, `END DATE`)

Group_names <- Dat_Group %>%
  group_keys() %>%
  mutate(Name_raw = paste(Category, EVENT, `START DATE`, sep = ""),
         Name = str_replace_all(Name_raw, '[/\\s\\$\\:\\*\\?"<>|]', "")) %>%
  pull(Name)

named_list <- Dat_Group %>%
  group_split() %>%
  set_names(Group_names)

# --- EXPORT: Use DISPLAY_START / DISPLAY_END in CSVs if user requested text output,
# but use true Dates for Excel writing to keep formatting reliable. ---
ExportList_exceltext <- map(named_list, ~ .x %>%
  mutate(DISPLAY_START_EXCEL = paste0('="', DISPLAY_START, '"'),
         DISPLAY_END_EXCEL   = paste0('="', DISPLAY_END, '"')) %>%
  select(USCID, DISPLAY_START_EXCEL, DISPLAY_END_EXCEL) %>%
  rename(`START DATE` = DISPLAY_START_EXCEL, `END DATE` = DISPLAY_END_EXCEL))

# Write per-group Excel files (using true Date objects for the worksheet)
for (nm in names(named_list)) {
  df <- named_list[[nm]] %>%
    # ensure true Date objects for writeData (use EventDateStart/EventDateEnd)
    mutate(
      START_DATE_FOR_XL = as.Date(EventDateStart, tryFormats = c("%Y-%m-%d", "%m/%d/%Y", "%m-%d-%Y")),
      END_DATE_FOR_XL   = as.Date(EventDateEnd, tryFormats = c("%Y-%m-%d", "%m/%d/%Y", "%m-%d-%Y"))
    ) %>%
    select(USCID, START_DATE_FOR_XL, END_DATE_FOR_XL) %>%
    rename(`START DATE` = START_DATE_FOR_XL, `END DATE` = END_DATE_FOR_XL)

  wb <- createWorkbook()
  addWorksheet(wb, "Sheet1")
  writeData(wb, "Sheet1", df)

  # Create style to show two-digit year
  two_digit_style <- createStyle(numFmt = "mm/dd/yyyy")

  # Apply style to START and END columns (col 2 and 3 here)
  if (nrow(df) > 0) {
    addStyle(wb, "Sheet1", style = two_digit_style, cols = 2, rows = 2:(nrow(df) + 1), gridExpand = TRUE)
    addStyle(wb, "Sheet1", style = two_digit_style, cols = 3, rows = 2:(nrow(df) + 1), gridExpand = TRUE)
  }

  out_xlsx <- file.path(out_dir, paste0(nm, ".xlsx"))
  saveWorkbook(wb, out_xlsx, overwrite = TRUE)
}

# Optional: write the text/CSV-style exports that preserve the textual display (if needed)
# Example: write all ExportList_exceltext into a single multi-sheet workbook or individual CSVs.
for (nm in names(ExportList_exceltext)) {
  df_txt <- ExportList_exceltext[[nm]]
  # write as CSV to preserve the '="...") trick for Excel textual display
  out_csv <- file.path(out_dir, paste0(nm, "_text.csv"))
  write_csv(df_txt, out_csv)
}

```
